name: VENTRICULOPERITONEAL SHUNT MALFUNCTION
definition: CSF escape without complete break / disconnection rare granulomatous reaction adjacent to shunt tube within / near ventricle proximal shunt failure from ventricular collapse

demographics.Age: usually seen in children > 3 years of age with relatively fixed head size

discriminating_features: sutural diastasis + increased size of cranial cavity
preferential enlargement of temporal horns in infants

general: sutural diastasis + increased size of cranial cavity
preferential enlargement of temporal horns in infants
shuntogram (by scintigram / contrast radiography) determines site of obstruction
brain edema tracking along shunt + within interstices of centrum semiovale (with partial
formation of white matter cyst surrounding ventricular catheter
NUC: no uptake within ventricles + normal peritoneal activity (= proximal obstruction)
collection of contrast material at peritoneal end of shunt without spillage (= distal
shunt tubing coiled in an abdominal soft-tissue mass
ventriculitis (= enlarged ventricles with irregular enhancing ventricular wall ± septations)
meningitis (= enhancement of cerebral cortical sulci)
irregular contrast-enhancing mass along course of shunt tube

(1) no enlargement with scarring of ventricular walls
clinical.Prognosis: small hematomas resolve on their own
